reforms.description: In 1968, the Portuguese government introduced new incentives for private fixed investment, including reductions in industrial tax on capital goods and more favorable depreciation allowances.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These incentives are designed to stimulate private sector investment, which can enhance productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The various incentives introduced for fixed investment in 1968 will remain in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of these incentives represents a significant policy shift aimed at enhancing private investment, which is crucial for economic development.
reforms.source_quote: The various incentives introduced for fixed investment in 1968 will remain in force.
